rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no amounts present and clearly a header.
Clear title under the year. Full phrase legible; no monetary columns aligned with it.
Left-column line reading 'Civitati Oxon' (handwritten Latin abbreviation). No amount immediately adjacent in the numeric columns that can be unambiguously assigned to this line.
Handwritten; read as 'Curiae Hastings' (could be 'Curice' or 'Curiae'). No clear amount aligned to this single line; description legible but slightly ambiguous.
Handwritten phrase appears to be 'Oriel Windows' followed by a short abbreviation 'B D ft' or similar. Faded / cramped; no clear numeric alignment for this specific line.
Handwritten, uncertain reading ('Adis Ni Bursar' or 'Aldis Ni Bursar'). No distinct amount aligned; transcription uncertain.
Relatively clear Latin abbreviation 'Coll Omn. Anim:' (collector of all souls?). No numeric entry directly aligned for this line.
Short item 'Coll Ball:' clearly visible but no obvious numeric value adjacent.
Handwritten abbreviation; reads as 'Coll Di. Jo: Bapt.' (some contraction). No amount clearly aligned to this single line.
Short, faint handwriting; read tentatively as 'Adig Tapors' or 'Adig Tapers'. No clear monetary columns adjacent.
Word reads clearly as 'Concionator' (Latin for preacher). No amount on the same horizontal line; the stacked numeric block for vicars follows below.

Line 'Vicario Morton' clearly visible. The monetary values for this heading are written in a vertical list to the right (four separate monetary lines), so this line is the descriptive entry for the following amount-lines.
First of four numeric lines to the right of 'Vicario Morton': clearly '31.19.2' with digits well aligned in the three monetary columns. Shillings (19) and pence (2) are within valid ranges.
Second numeric line for 'Vicario Morton': identical and clearly written '31.19.2'. Alignment and digits clear.
Third numeric line for 'Vicario Morton': clearly '32.9.11'. All currency values valid and digits legible.
Fourth numeric line for 'Vicario Morton': clearly '32.1.10'. Alignment with monetary columns is unambiguous.
Underlined sum for the four 'Vicario Morton' lines; reads cleanly as '128.2.1' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. This is clearly a calculated subtotal/total line.
Heading 'Vicario South Newton' is clear; the monetary lines for this heading appear immediately to the right in four stacked numeric lines (see following rows).
First monetary line for 'Vicario South Newton' reads well as '9.16.8'. Clear alignment with monetary columns.
Second identical monetary line for 'Vicario South Newton': '9.16.8' repeated; digits and alignment clear.
Third monetary line for 'Vicario South Newton' reads '9.17.6' clearly in the three columns.
Fourth monetary line for 'Vicario South Newton': also '9.17.6' and clearly legible.
Underlined subtotal for the four 'Vicario South Newton' lines; reads '39.8.4' cleanly in the monetary columns.
The word 'Insurances' (or 'Insurance') is written on the left and a monetary amount directly to its right reads clearly as '9.11.3' in the three columns. Alignment and digits are legible.

Left text is faded and abbreviated; read tentatively as 'Bampton do. - 18.6. B--- 3.2.' The monetary triple to the right (7.16.6) is taken from the aligned numeric column but the association with this exact description is somewhat uncertain due to cramped handwriting and abbreviations.

Handwritten left text reads as 'Benwar do 14.6 Do 2.5' (could be 'Bewar' or 'Bensur'). The monetary triple aligned to this line appears to be '2.5.0' but the left-to-right alignment is slightly uncertain; transcription of the text is low-confidence.

The left text explicitly includes the amount '2.1.0' as part of the description area ('Fridge/Fringe farm 2.1.0') and this reading is clear. Left-side amount printed next to the description used directly.
Left text 'Kidlington coats x' (or 'Kidlington coats &c') is readable and the amount to the right is clearly '20.0.0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Left-hand phrase uncertain (read as 'Fallonur 14. Intt' or similar). The numeric triple aligned to this line reads '7.4.6' clearly, but the exact textual transcription is low-confidence.
'Subscriptions Infirmary' is readable and the amount to the right is clearly '6.6.0'. Alignment is clear.
Indented item under subscriptions reads 'Kidlington School' and the aligned amount is clearly '5.5.0' in the monetary columns.
Indented line '2. Pew do' (i.e., two pews) is legible and the aligned amount to the right is '6.0.0' clearly written.
Indented line 'Do Curate' is clear; amount aligned to the right reads '15.0.0' unambiguously.
Final page total at bottom right is clearly underlined and reads '306.9.2' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Alignment and digits are unambiguous.
